LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFFS RONNIE FIELDS, CINDY CASTILLO, DARNELL CARTER, RUTH BARNETT AND CHARITY OWENS' MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION IS GRANTED. THE CLASS IS DEFINED AS ALL PERSONS WHO WERE OCCUPANTS OF THE FAIRFAX HOTEL FROM AUGUST 14, 2013 THROUGH MAY 31, 2014 WHO HAD RESIDED AT THE HOTEL FOR AT LEAST TWENTY CONSECUTIVE DAYS AND WERE REQUIRED TO MOVE, OR TO CHECK OUT AND RE-REGISTER, BEFORE THE 30TH CONSECUTIVE DAY OF THEIR RESIDENCY AT THE HOTEL. GIVEN THIS RE-FORMULATION OF THE CLASS DEFINITION, PLAINTIFFS HAVE SATISFIED ALL THE REQUIREMENTS FOR CERTIFICATION OF THE CLASS. ALL PLAINTIFFS EXCEPT MR. MCDONALD ARE APPOINTED CLASS REPRESENTATIVES. THE HOOSHMAND LAW GROUP IS APPOINTED CLASS COUNSEL. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. Notice Of Defendants Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Plaintiffs Motion For Class Certification
Off Calendar Dec-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS JASHVANT PATEL and HANSABEN PATEL'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Plaintiffs Motion For Class Certification is denied. Although the defendants have not provided any new or different facts, circumstances or law that they did not or could have raised previously, the court nonetheless took the opportunity afforded by this motion to revisit its earlier ruling and confirms that the earlier ruling should not be disturbed. All of defendants' arguments opposing class certification lack merit. The common question of liability predominates. The class definition is workable and does not contravene California class action principles. "A class is ascertainable even if the class definition pleads ultimate facts or conclusions of law." (Hicks v. Kaufman and Broad Home Corp. (2001) 89 Cal. App. 4th 908, 915). Class definition can be dependent on a subsequent determination of the validity of the class claim. (Ghazaryan v. Diva Limousine, Ltd. (2008) 169 Cal. App. 4th 1531). Here, class treatment is superior to any other form of adjudication. Order signed. RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Ronnie Fields, Cindy Castillo, Darnell Carter, Ruth Barnett And Charity Owens' Motion For Class Certification is granted. The class is defined as all persons who were occupants of the Fairfax Hotel from August 14, 2013 through May 31, 2014 who had resided at the Hotel for at least twenty consecutive days and were required to move, or to check out and re-register, before the 30th consecutive day of their residency at the Hotel. Given this re-formulation of the class definition, plaintiffs have satisfied all the requirements for certification of the class. All plaintiffs except Mr. McDonald are appointed class representatives. The Hooshmand Law Group is appointed class counsel. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
